Now, would they be, see, so you're always making these decisions, so like this is like sending them the audio when they're in a telecoaching program, right? As soon as they know you're doing it, attendance on the calls goes down. And now you're in to trying to figure out how the hell do we get them on the calls, right? What are the extra lures to be on the calls? But if you don't give them that, they like bail and mess. So you don't have any choice. But I wouldn't go beyond it where you had choice. So like I think giving them summaries, see what I would rather do, which we try and do, is write a little copy about what's on it that would make you want to hear it, right? Now, if we deliver the CD of the call to you and you ain't using that, and all you're using is the 4x6 card with the summary on it, 200 bucks a month's a lot of money for a 4x6 card, right? They learn better by reading than listening. I personally prefer it because I'm never in a car now and I don't want to be bothered. I'm not an iPod guy, you know, but so I'm happier to have the thing and highlight it, and you know, so I don't think that's as harmful. But I think making it easy not to use the thing you're delivering to them is a mistake. Okay, I gotta do an exercise before we run out of time.
